mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in falling corporate revenues.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation indicates real rate compression, boosting equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample 20-day return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in total nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate consumer spending capacity, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines correlate with positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of monthly observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP level exceeding 20000 billion chained dollars signals robust economic expansion, prompting immediate algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns following GDPC1 releases above 20000 are negative in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during a recessionary phase confirms demand destruction, forcing equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in recession samples.
